title: Company-specific employee records
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want each employee record to be scoped to exactly one company, so that workforce data remains isolated and accurate per company.
acceptance_criteria: Given I create an employee, when the record is saved, then it is associated with exactly one company and cannot be shared across companies.; Given the same physical person is employed by two or more companies, when records are created, then a separate, independent employee record exists in each company rather than a single shared record.; Given a physical person already has an employee record in one company, when I create their record in another company, then the new record is created without merging or overwriting the existing one.; Given employee records exist in multiple companies for one person, when I query a company, then only that company's employee records are returned.

title: Employee with or without a linked user account
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to maintain an employee record either standalone or linked to a user account, so that I can track staff who do not need system access as well as those who do.
acceptance_criteria: Given I create an employee, when no system access is required, then the record can be saved without any linked user account.; Given an employee requires system access, when I link a user account, then the employee record is associated with exactly that account.; Given a standalone (non-user) employee later needs access, when I link a user account, then the existing record is preserved and access is enabled without creating a duplicate employee.; Given a linked employee no longer needs access, when the user account is unlinked or deactivated, then the employee record itself remains intact.

title: Core organizational assignments
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to assign each employee to a company, jurisdiction, department(s), and position, so that their placement in the organization is clearly defined.
acceptance_criteria: Given I create or edit an employee, when I save the record, then exactly one company, exactly one jurisdiction, and exactly one position are assigned.; Given an employee record, when I assign departments, then at least one department is mandatory and multiple departments may be assigned.; Given I attempt to save without a jurisdiction, position, or at least one department, when I submit, then the system blocks the save and prompts for the missing mandatory assignment.; Given an employee belongs to multiple departments, when I view the record, then all department memberships are displayed.

title: Group and multi-location assignment
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to optionally assign employees to groups and to one or more operating locations, so that I can reflect team membership and where staff operate.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee record, when I assign groups, then zero or more groups may be linked without any group being mandatory.; Given an employee who works across sites, when I assign locations, then one or more operating locations may be recorded.; Given an employee operates at multiple locations, when I view the record, then all assigned locations are displayed.; Given no group is assigned, when I save the record, then the save succeeds because groups are optional.

title: Employee lifecycle events
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want employee records to support lifecycle events such as onboarding, probation, performance, transfers, and exit, so that I can manage the full employment journey in one place.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee record, when an onboarding, probation, performance, transfer, or exit event occurs, then each such lifecycle event can be captured against the record.; Given lifecycle events are recorded, when I review an employee, then their current lifecycle stage is clearly visible.; Given a transfer event, when it is captured, then the resulting organizational changes (e.g., company/department/position) are reflected on the record.; Given an exit event, when it is processed, then the employee's status reflects exit while historical lifecycle data is retained.

title: Primary reporting manager
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want each employee to have exactly one primary reporting manager, so that the core reporting structure is unambiguous.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee record, when I set the reporting structure, then exactly one primary manager is assigned.; Given an employee, when no primary manager is set, then the system enforces that a single primary manager is required.; Given a primary manager is already set, when I attempt to assign a second primary manager, then the system prevents more than one primary manager.; Given a primary manager is changed, when I save, then the previous primary manager is replaced by exactly one new primary manager.

title: Dotted-line (matrix) managers
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to optionally assign one or more functional/matrix (dotted-line) managers to an employee, so that dotted-line reporting relationships are represented.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee record, when I add dotted-line managers, then zero, one, or more functional managers may be assigned.; Given dotted-line managers exist, when I view the employee, then they are shown as distinct from the primary manager.; Given no dotted-line manager is assigned, when I save, then the save succeeds because dotted-line managers are optional.; Given a dotted-line manager assignment, when it is added or removed, then it does not affect the single primary manager relationship.

title: Temporary/acting manager with automatic delegation
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to assign a temporary/acting manager for an employee's team, so that management responsibilities are delegated automatically while the primary manager is unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee, when a temporary/acting manager is assigned, then the assignment is recorded against the employee.; Given a temporary manager assignment is active, when it is in effect, then delegation of the manager's responsibilities to the acting manager occurs automatically.; Given a temporary manager assignment has an end, when the period lapses, then delegation reverts to the primary manager automatically.; Given a temporary/acting assignment, when it is created, then it does not overwrite the recorded primary manager.

title: Effective-dated manager changes with audit trail
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want all manager assignment changes (primary, dotted-line, and temporary/acting) to be effective-dated with a complete audit trail, so that reporting history is accurate and traceable.
acceptance_criteria: Given I change any manager assignment, when I save it, then an effective date is required.; Given manager changes have been made over time, when I review history, then a complete audit trail of each change (who, what, when, effective date) is available.; Given an effective-dated future change, when the effective date arrives, then the new assignment takes effect on that date.; Given historical manager assignments, when I look back at any point in time, then the manager relationship in effect on that date is retrievable.

title: Duplicate detection on government IDs
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the system to enforce uniqueness of government identification fields (Aadhar, Passport, PAN) with deduplication prompts, so that duplicate employee records are prevented.
acceptance_criteria: Given I enter an Aadhar, Passport, or PAN, when the value already exists, then the system flags it and prompts for deduplication.; Given a duplicate government ID is detected, when I proceed, then I am guided to resolve the duplicate before the record can be saved.; Given uniqueness is enforced per government ID field, when any of Aadhar, Passport, or PAN collides, then the specific conflicting field is identified in the prompt.; Given the same person legitimately works for multiple companies, when their government ID recurs, then deduplication guidance distinguishes a same-company duplicate from a valid separate-company record.

title: Statutory workforce data capture (Indian labor law)
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to capture statutory workforce data such as UAN, ESIC number, ESI/PF eligibility, Professional Tax registration, LWF applicability, maternity benefit eligibility, and gratuity eligibility, so that the company remains compliant with Indian labor law.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee record, when I enter statutory details, then UAN, ESIC number, ESI/PF eligibility status, Professional Tax registration, LWF applicability, maternity benefit eligibility, and gratuity eligibility tracking can all be captured.; Given statutory fields are captured, when I view the employee, then all these details are available for compliance reporting.; Given gratuity eligibility, when service duration or criteria change, then eligibility can be tracked over time.; Given ESI/PF eligibility status, when it is set, then it drives applicability of related statutory contributions for the employee.

title: Leave balances with statutory entitlements
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to track employee leave balances with their statutory entitlements, so that leave records reflect legally mandated allowances.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee record, when leave data is maintained, then leave balances are tracked alongside their statutory entitlements.; Given leave balances exist, when I review an employee, then current balances and statutory entitlements are visible.; Given statutory entitlements apply, when balances are computed, then they reflect the legally mandated allowances for the employee's jurisdiction.

title: Self-service view of own employee profile
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to view my own employee profile including my organizational placement, so that I can confirm my company, jurisdiction, department(s), position, groups, and locations are correct.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am a linked user with an employee record, when I open my profile, then my company, jurisdiction, department(s), position, groups, and operating location(s) are displayed.; Given I view my profile, when data spans multiple departments or locations, then all memberships are shown.; Given I belong to more than one company as separate employee records, when I view my profile in a company context, then only that company's record is shown.; Given I am viewing my own record, when I access it, then I can see but not modify company-controlled organizational assignments.

title: Self-service view of own reporting structure
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to view my primary, dotted-line, and any temporary/acting managers, so that I understand my reporting lines.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am a linked user, when I view my reporting structure, then my single primary manager is displayed distinctly.; Given I have dotted-line managers, when I view my reporting structure, then all functional/matrix managers are listed separately from the primary manager.; Given a temporary/acting manager is active for me, when I view my reporting structure, then the acting manager and the period are shown.; Given my manager assignments have changed, when I view current reporting, then the assignment effective as of today is shown.

title: Self-service view of own leave balances and statutory data
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to view my leave balances, statutory entitlements, and my statutory identifiers, so that I can verify my compliance-related information.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am a linked user, when I open my leave section, then my current leave balances and statutory entitlements are displayed.; Given I view my statutory data, when it exists, then my UAN, ESIC number, ESI/PF eligibility, Professional Tax, LWF, maternity, and gratuity eligibility are visible to me.; Given statutory identifiers are sensitive, when displayed, then values are shown to me for my own record only.; Given a discrepancy, when I identify one, then I can view (read-only) the data so it can be raised for correction.

title: Consolidated employee oversight across group companies
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want a consolidated view of employee records across the companies in my group, so that I can oversee the workforce while records remain company-specific.
acceptance_criteria: Given I administer multiple companies in a group, when I view employees, then I can see records across all companies in my group.; Given records are company-specific, when the same person is employed by two group companies, then I see two distinct employee records, one per company.; Given I view a specific employee, when I open the record, then its company scope is clearly indicated.; Given cross-company oversight, when I filter or search, then results respect each company's data boundaries.

title: Records maintained for staff without system access
user_story: As an Employee (Non-User), I want my employment, organizational, statutory, and reporting data to be fully maintained on my behalf without a user account, so that I am tracked and compliant even though I have no system access.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have no linked user account, when my record is created, then all mandatory data (company, jurisdiction, department, position, primary manager, statutory data) can be captured on my behalf.; Given I am a non-user employee, when the record exists, then I have no self-service login while my data remains complete and compliant.; Given I later require access, when a user account is linked, then my existing record is preserved without duplication.; Given lifecycle events occur, when I am a non-user, then onboarding, probation, transfers, and exit are still recorded against my record.

title: Manager delegation during absence
user_story: As an Employee (User) who is a manager, I want an acting manager to receive my responsibilities automatically during my absence, so that my team is managed continuously while I am away.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am a manager going on leave, when a temporary/acting manager is set for my reports, then the assignment is effective-dated and recorded in the audit trail.; Given the acting assignment is active, when approvals or manager actions arise, then they are automatically delegated to the acting manager.; Given the delegation period ends, when I return, then responsibilities revert to me automatically.; Given the delegation, when it is in effect, then my recorded primary-manager relationship with my reports is not overwritten.

title: Jurisdiction statutory rule-packs for workforce data
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to maintain effective-dated, versioned jurisdiction rule-packs that define which statutory data applies (UAN, ESIC, ESI/PF, Professional Tax, LWF, maternity, gratuity) and their entitlement parameters, so that the engines determine compliance per jurisdiction without code changes.
acceptance_criteria: Given a jurisdiction (e.g., Indian labor law), when I configure its rule-pack, then the applicable statutory fields, eligibility thresholds, and entitlement parameters are defined as governed config.; Given a rule-pack, when I publish a new version, then it is versioned and effective-dated so prior determinations remain reproducible.; Given a rule-pack is updated, when the change is saved, then no code deployment is required for engines to apply it.; Given an employee in a jurisdiction, when their statutory data is evaluated, then the engine reads the effective rule-pack for that jurisdiction as of the relevant date.

title: Duplicate-detection uniqueness configuration
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to configure which government identification fields (Aadhar, Passport, PAN) enforce uniqueness and how matching/dedup scope is evaluated, so that duplicate-detection behavior is governed config rather than hard-coded.
acceptance_criteria: Given the duplicate-detection config, when I define uniqueness rules, then each government ID field can be flagged as unique and scoped (e.g., within-company vs cross-company).; Given the config is versioned and effective-dated, when I change which fields enforce uniqueness, then the change applies without a code release.; Given a matching decision table, when it is configured, then it specifies how a same-company duplicate is distinguished from a valid separate-company record.; Given the config exists, when the engine performs dedup, then it reads these rules to decide flag vs allow.

title: Jurisdiction statutory field schema (dynamic fields)
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to define the statutory/custom field schema per jurisdiction (labels, data types, required/optional, validation) as governed metadata, so that the employee form renders the correct statutory fields for each jurisdiction without code changes.
acceptance_criteria: Given a jurisdiction, when I define its statutory field schema, then field definitions (type, validation, mandatory status, ordering) are stored as versioned, effective-dated metadata.; Given the schema is updated, when the change is published, then the employee capture form reflects it without code deployment.; Given differing jurisdictions, when their schemas differ, then each jurisdiction can present a distinct set of statutory fields.; Given a mandatory statutory field in the schema, when a record is captured in that jurisdiction, then the field is enforced per the config.

title: Lifecycle stage and event configuration
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure lifecycle stages and their parameters (e.g., probation duration, transfer and exit checklists) as governed config, so that the employee lifecycle behaves per company policy without code changes.
acceptance_criteria: Given lifecycle config, when I define stages such as onboarding, probation, transfer, and exit, then their parameters (e.g., probation length, required steps) are captured as versioned config.; Given a probation duration is configured, when an employee enters probation, then the stage uses the configured duration.; Given the config is effective-dated, when policy changes, then new records follow the updated config while existing history is preserved.; Given lifecycle config is changed, when saved, then no code deployment is required.

title: Automatic statutory eligibility determination (Rules engine)
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the Rules engine to automatically determine ESI/PF, maternity, and gratuity eligibility by evaluating the employee's data against the jurisdiction rule-pack decision tables, so that eligibility is consistent and audit-traceable.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee's compensation, service, and jurisdiction data, when the Rules engine evaluates the applicable decision tables, then ESI/PF, maternity, and gratuity eligibility are determined automatically.; Given an eligibility outcome, when it is computed, then the rule-pack version and inputs used are recorded for auditability.; Given service duration or criteria change, when re-evaluated, then eligibility is recomputed and effective-dated.; Given the rule-pack changes, when the engine next evaluates, then the new decision tables are applied without code changes.

title: Leave balance accrual with statutory entitlements (Accrual engine)
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the Accrual/Balance engine to compute and maintain leave balances from statutory entitlements defined in the jurisdiction rule-pack, so that balances reflect legally mandated allowances automatically.
acceptance_criteria: Given statutory entitlement parameters in the jurisdiction rule-pack, when the Accrual engine runs, then leave balances are computed per the mandated allowances for the employee's jurisdiction.; Given accrual events over time, when balances change, then the engine updates running balances with an auditable history.; Given a jurisdiction with different entitlements, when balances are computed, then the correct jurisdiction-specific allowances are applied.; Given entitlement config changes, when the engine next accrues, then updated parameters are used without code changes.

title: Automatic delegation routing on acting-manager assignment (Workflow engine)
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the Workflow/Approval engine to automatically reroute a manager's pending and incoming approvals to the temporary/acting manager while the assignment is active, so that approvals are not blocked during absence.
acceptance_criteria: Given an active temporary/acting manager assignment, when approval tasks are routed, then the Workflow engine directs the primary manager's items to the acting manager automatically.; Given the assignment period lapses, when the engine next routes, then approvals revert to the primary manager automatically.; Given delegated actions occur, when they are taken by the acting manager, then the audit trail records the delegation context.; Given the primary manager relationship, when delegation is active, then routing changes do not overwrite the recorded primary manager.

title: Duplicate matching evaluation (Rules engine)
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the Rules engine to evaluate government IDs against the configured duplicate-detection decision tables and classify each hit, so that same-company duplicates are blocked while valid separate-company records are allowed.
acceptance_criteria: Given a government ID is entered, when the Rules engine evaluates it against the configured decision table, then it returns a match classification (same-company duplicate vs separate-company allowed).; Given a same-company duplicate is classified, when the result returns, then the engine flags the specific conflicting field and blocks save until resolved.; Given a valid separate-company recurrence, when classified, then the engine permits the record per config.; Given the uniqueness config changes, when the engine next evaluates, then the updated rules are applied without code changes.

title: Lifecycle and manager-change notifications (Notification engine)
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want the Notification/Template engine to notify me and affected managers of lifecycle events (e.g., probation ending, transfer, exit) and manager-assignment changes using configured templates, so that stakeholders stay informed automatically.
acceptance_criteria: Given a lifecycle event or manager-assignment change occurs, when it is committed, then the Notification engine sends notifications rendered from the configured templates.; Given a probation stage is ending, when the threshold is reached, then the relevant parties are notified per template config.; Given a manager change becomes effective, when it takes effect, then the employee and impacted managers are notified.; Given templates are updated in config, when the engine next sends, then the new template content is used without code changes.

title: Metadata-driven employee directory search and grid
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a metadata-driven employee directory with search, filtering, and configurable columns across organizational attributes, so that I can find and manage employee records efficiently within my company scope.
acceptance_criteria: Given the employee directory, when I search or filter by company, jurisdiction, department, position, group, location, or manager, then matching records are returned within my authorized scope.; Given the grid is metadata-driven, when column and filter configuration changes, then the UI reflects it without code changes.; Given tenant-scoped access, when I browse, then only records I am authorized to see are shown.; Given an employee has multiple departments or locations, when displayed in the grid, then those multi-value attributes are represented correctly.
